title: What to know about Vitis vinifera before using it medicinally
uses_description: The main medicinal uses of Vitis vinifera are alleviating cardiovascular disease through its flavonoids and phenolic acids, which possess antioxidant properties that help reduce inflammation and improve cardiovascular health. Vitis vinifera extracts have also been shown to regulate blood sugar levels and improve insulin sensitivity in individuals with diabetes, making it a potential natural remedy for this condition. Its proanthocyanidins have been found to inhibit cancer cell growth and induce apoptosis in cancer cells, suggesting its potential application in cancer treatment. Additionally, the plant's anti-inflammatory properties, attributed to its resveratrol content, can help alleviate arthritis symptoms by reducing joint inflammation and pain. Furthermore, Vitis vinifera has been used to treat digestive issues, including diarrhea, through its prebiotic and antimicrobial properties, which can help regulate gut health and alleviate symptoms of diarrhea, inflammation, and fever, while its flavonoids also help lower blood pressure and alleviate cough.
properties_desc: The medicinal properties of Vitis vinifera are attributed to its antioxidant activity, which is mainly due to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids. This plant also exhibits anti-inflammatory properties, thanks to the saponins and oligomers, which can help in reducing inflammation and alleviating pain, thereby acting as an analgesic. The antimicrobial properties of Vitis vinifera are attributed to the volatile oils, which can inhibit the growth of various microorganisms, while its antihistaminic properties are due to the presence of flavonoids and terpenoids, which can help in alleviating allergic reactions. Furthermore, the anticancer properties of Vitis vinifera are attributed to the presence of resveratrol and other polyphenolic compounds, which can help in inhibiting the growth of cancer cells and inducing apoptosis. Additionally, the bioactive compounds present in Vitis vinifera, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, can also exhibit antiviral, hypoglycemic, and vasodilator properties, thereby making it a valuable medicinal plant.
identification: Vitis vinifera is a deciduous woody vine with perennial woody stems and a climber habit, native to the Old World, specifically the regions of Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. The plant's leaves are simple, alternate, and lobed with 3-5 leaflets, typically 10-20 cm long and 5-15 cm wide, with a cleft at the base and an entire margin. It produces small, hermaphroditic flowers in clusters, known as a corymb, which are yellowish-green in color and arranged in a spiral pattern. Vitis vinifera is widely cultivated in many parts of the world, including the Americas, Australia, and southern Africa, for its fruit and uses in winemaking, with suitable climates and elevations varying from sea level to 1,000 meters. Its growth habit and fruit production are influenced by factors such as temperature, sunlight, soil quality, and water availability.
cultivation: Vitis vinifera is a deciduous climbing vine that requires a trellis or other support to grow, with a growth habit that can be trained to a variety of forms including a central leader system, a cordons, and a lyre system. The optimal growth requirements include a full sun exposure, a well-drained soil with a pH between 5.5 and 6.5, and an annual precipitation of at least 600 mm. To promote healthy growth, it is recommended to prune the vine annually in late winter or early spring, removing any dead, diseased, or damaged wood, and to provide a balanced fertilizer during the growing season. Regular monitoring of the vine's water and nutrient needs is also crucial, as well as protection from pests and diseases such as powdery mildew and downy mildew. By providing the right conditions and care, Vitis vinifera can thrive and produce high-quality fruit.
history: Vitis vinifera is an ancient medicinal plant used by various cultures to treat a range of ailments. The ancient Greeks and Romans valued its antiseptic properties, using it to treat wounds and infections. In traditional Chinese medicine, Vitis vinifera is used to treat fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The plant's flavonoids and phenolic acids have been shown to exhibit antimicrobial and antiviral activity, making it useful for treating colds and flu. Archaeological evidence suggests that Vitis vinifera was also used by ancient Egyptians to treat eye and skin disorders.

constituents.name: Quercetin
constituents.description: Quercetin is a flavonoid antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral constituent of Vitis vinifera.

constituents.name: Resveratrol
constituents.description: A stilbenoid antioxidant that has anti-inflammatory, antiviral, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, as well as the ability to inhibit platelet aggregation and promote vasodilation.

constituents.name: Catechins
constituents.description: Catechins are a type of polyphenolic compound with antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties.

constituents.name: Kaempferol
constituents.description: Kaempferol is a flavonoid with antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties.

constituents.name: Phenolic acids
constituents.description: Phenolic acids are a class of compounds found in Vitis vinifera with antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties

constituents.name: Saponins
constituents.description: Saponins are triterpenoid glycosides found in the fruit and leaves of Vitis vinifera, having foaming properties, hemolytic activity, and cytotoxic effects.

constituents.name: Epicatechin
constituents.description: Epicatechin is a polyphenolic compound found in Vitis vinifera, known for its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and cardiovascular-protective properties.

constituents.name: Ferulic acid
constituents.description: Ferulic acid is a polyphenolic compound with antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties.

constituents.name: Flavonoids
constituents.description: Flavonoids are a class of compounds found in Vitis vinifera that exhibit antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and free radical-scavenging properties.

constituents.name: Tannins
constituents.description: Polyphenolic compounds found in Vitis vinifera with astringent, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties.

constituents.name: Anthocyanins
constituents.description: Anthocyanins are powerful, water-soluble pigments with antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, capable of scavenging free radicals and neutralizing oxidative stress.

constituents.name: Tyrosol
constituents.description: Tyrosol is a phenolic compound with antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.

constituents.name: Gallic acid
constituents.description: A polyphenolic compound found in Vitis vinifera, exhibiting antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and anticancer properties.

constituents.name: Phloridzin
constituents.description: Phloridzin is a flavonoid glycoside with antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and potential cardioprotective properties.

constituents.name: Phenolic Acids
constituents.description: Phenolic Acids are a class of compounds found in Vitis vinifera, characterized by their antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties.

constituents.name: Fisetin
constituents.description: Fisetin is a flavonoid antioxidant with anti-inflammatory, anti-cancer, and anti-aging properties.

constituents.name: Quercitrin
constituents.description: Quercitrin is a flavonoid glycoside with antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties.

constituents.name: Ellagic acid
constituents.description: A polyphenolic compound with antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, found in the skin, leaves, and fruit of Vitis vinifera.

constituents.name: Ferulic Acid
constituents.description: Ferulic Acid is a polyphenolic antioxidant with anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and photoprotective properties.

constituents.name: Gallocatechin
constituents.description: Gallocatechin is a flavan-3-ol found in Vitis vinifera, with antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties.

side_effects.side_effect_list_description: Contact urticaria and anaphylaxis occur due to Vitis vinifera allergen
Increased risk of sunburn and skin damage due to heightened sensitivity to ultraviolet light.
Ingestion of Vitis vinifera extracts causes gastrointestinal irritation and inflammation
Ingestion of Vitis vinifera may exacerbate bleeding disorders or interact with anticoagulant medications, potentially leading to increased bleeding risk.
Vitis vinifera contains falcarinol, a compound that can cause a significant decrease in platelet count leading to thrombocytopenia
Concomitant use of Vitis vinifera with warfarin may increase the risk of bleeding due to decreased warfarin metabolism by grapefruit juice, a constituent of Vitis vinifera.
Vitis vinifera contains oxalate crystals which can cause renal injury and interstitial nephritis
Consuming Vitis vinifera may cause gastrointestinal irritation leading to nausea and vomiting in some individuals due to the presence of tartaric acid and other compounds.
Long-term excessive consumption of Vitis vinifera extracts leads to hepatotoxicity, characterized by necrosis and fibrosis of liver cells.
Vitis vinifera extracts cause a significant decrease in systolic blood pressure

uses_list.description: It has been found that extracts from this plant have antidiabetic properties, reducing blood sugar levels and improving insulin sensitivity, which may help manage diabetes symptoms.
Studies have shown that compounds found in this plant have a blood pressure-lowering effect, which may help manage hypertension symptoms.
The plant contains anti-inflammatory compounds that may help reduce inflammation and alleviate associated symptoms.
Its extracts have been found to have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which may help alleviate arthritis symptoms such as pain and inflammation.
Compounds found in this plant have been shown to have anticancer properties, inhibiting the growth of cancer cells and inducing apoptosis, which may help manage cancer symptoms.
Its extracts have been found to have a cholesterol-lowering effect and improve cardiovascular health, which may help manage atherosclerosis symptoms.
The plant's extracts have been found to have anti-inflammatory and protective effects on the stomach lining, which may help alleviate gastritis symptoms.
Its extracts have been found to have anti-inflammatory and moisturizing properties, which may help alleviate eczema symptoms such as itching and dry skin.
Compounds found in this plant have been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help alleviate asthma symptoms such as inflammation and airway constriction.
Its extracts have been found to have a blood pressure-lowering effect, improve lipid profiles, and reduce inflammation, which may help manage cardiovascular disease symptoms.

uses_modern_western_medicine_description: In modern western medicine, Vitis vinifera is used to treat various conditions, including diabetes, by regulating blood sugar levels and improving insulin sensitivity. The plant's extracts have also shown potential in cancer treatment, particularly in inhibiting the growth of certain cancer cells. Furthermore, Vitis vinifera is used to alleviate atherosclerosis, a condition characterized by the buildup of plaque in arteries, by reducing inflammation and improving blood lipid profiles. Additionally, it has been found to be effective in managing hypertension, inflammation, cardiovascular disease, arthritis, high blood pressure, gastritis, inflammatory bowel disease, eczema, varicose veins, obesity, skin conditions, anxiety, Alzheimer's disease, high cholesterol, hypercholesterolemia, and hyperlipidemia, often by regulating lipid metabolism, reducing oxidative stress, and promoting antioxidant defenses. The plant's various bioactive compounds work synergistically to address these conditions, making it a valuable resource in modern western medicine.
uses_traditional_chinese_medicine_descri: In traditional Chinese medicine, Vitis vinifera is used to treat various health conditions, including hypertension by reducing blood pressure through its flavonoid compounds, diabetes by enhancing insulin sensitivity due to its antioxidant properties, gastritis by protecting the stomach lining with its polyphenols, atherosclerosis by inhibiting the formation of plaque in arteries with its flavonoids. The plant's anti-inflammatory properties also make it effective in treating inflammation, and its antioxidant and anti-cancer properties have been shown to prevent cancer, while its cardio-protective effects help in reducing the risk of cardiovascular disease. Furthermore, Vitis vinifera is used to alleviate symptoms of arthritis, asthma, eczema, gout, hyperlipidemia, varicose veins, rheumatism, and diarrhea due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Additionally, it has been found to be effective in reducing the severity of influenza, hepatitis, and inflammatory bowel disease. Its antioxidant properties also help in reducing anxiety and preventing the progression of cancer, making it a valuable treatment in traditional Chinese medicine.
uses_ayurvedic_medicine_description: In ayurvedic medicine, Vitis vinifera is used to treat various health conditions, including diabetes by regulating blood sugar levels, hypertension through its diuretic properties, and inflammation by reducing pro-inflammatory enzymes. The plant's antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties also help in managing arthritis, while its flavonoids and polyphenols have shown potential in inhibiting cancer cell growth and atherosclerosis. Additionally, Vitis vinifera is used to treat gastritis by soothing the stomach lining, asthma by relaxing bronchial muscles, and digestive issues by stimulating digestive enzymes. It is also effective in managing eczema, anxiety, and skin conditions due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, as well as rheumatism and gout through its analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects. Furthermore, the plant is used to treat gastroesophageal reflux disease, ulcers, varicose veins, gastrointestinal issues, skin infections, and other conditions, showcasing its broad therapeutic potential.
uses_unani_medicine_description: In unani medicine, Vitis vinifera is used to treat a variety of health conditions, including diabetes, where its polyphenolic compounds have been shown to possess antidiabetic properties. The berries of Vitis vinifera are also used to manage hypertension, as they contain potassium which helps to lower blood pressure. Furthermore, the plant is used to alleviate symptoms of asthma, asthma being a condition characterized by chronic inflammation of the airways, which can be treated with the anti-inflammatory properties of Vitis vinifera. Additionally, it is used to treat rheumatoid arthritis, a chronic autoimmune disorder, and eczema, a skin condition characterized by inflammation and skin lesions. The plant's antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties also make it effective in treating inflammation, skin cancer, gastritis, gout, osteoporosis, migraines, psoriasis, digestive issues, rheumatism, hyperlipidemia, atherosclerosis, anxiety, insomnia, high blood pressure, and skin infections, making it a valuable herb in unani medicine.
uses_homeopathic_medicine_description: In homeopathic medicine, Vitis vinifera is used to treat various health conditions. The plant's extract is known to help manage diabetes by improving insulin sensitivity, while its antihypertensive properties also make it effective in treating hypertension. Additionally, Vitis vinifera has been found to reduce inflammation, making it a valuable treatment for arthritis, chronic inflammation, atherosclerosis, and rheumatism. Furthermore, its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties help alleviate symptoms of eczema, psoriasis, and digestive issues, while also providing relief from gastritis and asthma. The plant's extracts have also been studied for their potential to prevent cancer, cardiovascular disease, high blood pressure, hypercholesterolemia, anxiety, varicose veins, osteoporosis, and inflammatory bowel disease.

benefits_list.description: This benefit is due to the presence of polyphenolic compounds, specifically resveratrol, which has been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines, thereby reducing inflammation in the body.

The plant contains a range of polyphenolic compounds, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been shown to scavenge free radicals and reduce oxidative stress in the body, thereby protecting cells from damage.
The plant contains polyphenolic compounds, specifically resveratrol, which has been shown to have vasodilatory effects, reducing blood pressure by relaxing blood vessels and improving blood flow.

The plant contains a range of compounds, including polyphenolic acids and flavonoids, which have been shown to exhibit antimicrobial activity against a range of bacteria and fungi, thereby preventing infection and promoting wound healing.

The plant contains a range of polyphenolic compounds, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been shown to have antioxidant properties, scavenging free radicals and reducing oxidative stress in the body.

The plant contains polyphenolic compounds, specifically resveratrol, which has been shown to inhibit the growth and proliferation of cancer cells, induce apoptosis, and reduce angiogenesis, thereby inhibiting tumor growth.

The plant contains polyphenolic compounds, specifically resveratrol, which has been shown to have anxiolytic effects, reducing anxiety and stress levels by regulating the activity of neurotransmitters in the brain.

The plant contains polyphenolic compounds, specifically resveratrol, which has been shown to improve insulin sensitivity by activating AMP-activated protein kinase, reducing inflammation, and improving glucose uptake in muscles.

The plant contains polyphenolic compounds, specifically resveratrol, which has been shown to have antiviral activity against a range of viruses, including HIV and herpes, by inhibiting viral replication and reducing the severity of symptoms.

The plant contains polyphenolic compounds, specifically resveratrol, which has been shown to have neuroprotective effects, reducing the risk of age-related cognitive decline and neurodegenerative diseases such as Alzheimer's and Parkinson's by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain.

benefits_description: Vitis vinifera reduces inflammation by suppressing the activity of pro-inflammatory enzymes, while also exhibiting antimicrobial action that fights off pathogens and supports brain health through its neuroprotective properties. The plant's ability to combat free radicals, which are unstable molecules that damage cells, showcases its antioxidant properties that protect against oxidative stress. Vitis vinifera lowers blood pressure by inhibiting the angiotensin-converting enzyme, which constricts blood vessels, and also inhibits cancer cells through its cytotoxic compounds that induce apoptosis. Additionally, the plant has antiviral activity that prevents viral replication, and its ability to improve insulin sensitivity helps manage glucose levels in individuals with type 2 diabetes. By exhibiting these diverse health benefits, Vitis vinifera also reduces anxiety levels by modulating the activity of neurotransmitters in the brain, and its cardiovascular benefits are further complemented by its ability to improve lipid profiles.
benefits_circulatory_system_description: Vitis vinifera lower blood pressure by containing compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help to relax blood vessels. The flavonoids present in Vitis vinifera prevent blood clots by inhibiting platelet aggregation, thus reducing the risk of cardiovascular diseases. The antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties of Vitis vinifera also reduce inflammation in the body, which is a major risk factor for cardiovascular diseases. Furthermore, Vitis vinifera improves circulation by dilating blood vessels and reducing blood pressure, thereby increasing oxygen delivery to tissues and organs. The flavonoids and other compounds in Vitis vinifera also regulate heart rate, reduce cholesterol, prevent atherosclerosis, increase nitric oxide production, enhance endothelial function, and reduce triglycerides, resulting in improved cardiovascular health.
benefits_digestive_system_description: Vitis vinifera relieves constipation due to its high fiber content and prebiotic properties, which help to stimulate bowel movement and improve the balance of gut microbiota. The plant's anti-inflammatory properties, resulting from its antioxidant and polyphenol content, reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, alleviating symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome and other inflammatory conditions. Vitis vinifera improves digestion by enhancing the absorption of nutrients and regulating bowel function, ensuring a more efficient breakdown and utilization of food. The antimicrobial properties of Vitis vinifera prevent diarrhea by inhibiting the growth of pathogenic bacteria, such as E. coli and Salmonella, in the gut. Vitis vinifera reduces bloating and alleviates nausea by soothing irritation in the digestive tract and regulating gut motility, while also healing ulcers in the stomach lining and small intestine.
benefits_endocrine_system_description: Vitis vinifera regulates hormones by containing flavonoids that modulate the endocrine system's activity. The plant reduces inflammation due to its anti-inflammatory properties, which in turn contribute to improved insulin sensitivity and glucose metabolism. The antimicrobial properties of Vitis vinifera also help boost testosterone levels in individuals with low testosterone. Moreover, it suppresses estrogen production through its anti-estrogenic activity, stabilizes the thyroid by regulating thyroid hormones and enhancing metabolism, controls cortisol levels by exerting a cortisol-reducing effect, supports adrenal function by acting as an adaptogen, and balances oestrogen levels through its phytoestrogenic activity.
benefits_integumentary_system_descriptio: Vitis vinifera protects skin from damage caused by UV radiation due to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids. Its anti-inflammatory properties reduce inflammation, soothing redness and discomfort, making it effective in treating skin conditions such as eczema and acne. Furthermore, the antimicrobial actions of Vitis vinifera fight fungal infections, including those caused by Candida albicans, by inhibiting the growth of microorganisms. The antioxidant effects of its polyphenols heal wounds by promoting tissue repair and regeneration, while its ability to remove toxins helps prevent the formation of scar tissue. Vitis vinifera also stimulates regeneration by promoting cell growth and differentiation, making it an effective treatment for skin irritations and damaged skin tissues.
benefits_lymphatic_system_description: Vitis vinifera boosts lymph flow by increasing the production of nitric oxide, a potent vasodilator, which relaxes smooth muscle cells and widens blood vessels, facilitating the movement of lymphatic fluid. Improved lymph drainage is achieved through the antioxidant properties of Vitis vinifera, which scavenge free radicals and reduce oxidative stress, promoting the removal of waste and toxins from the lymphatic system. Enhanced lymphatic filtration is facilitated by the anti-inflammatory properties of Vitis vinifera, which reduce inflammation and swelling in the lymph nodes, allowing for more efficient filtration of lymphatic fluid. Stimulation of lymph node growth is attributed to the antimicrobial properties of Vitis vinifera, which inhibit the growth of pathogenic microorganisms and promote a healthy immune response. Regular consumption of Vitis vinifera also reduces lymph swelling, fights lymph cancer cells, increases lymph protein production, supports lymph immune function, promotes lymph tissue repair, and enhances lymph circulation, ultimately contributing to a healthy and functioning lymphatic system.
benefits_musculoskeletal_system_descript: Vitis vinifera relieves muscle pain due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and neutralize free radicals that cause muscle damage. The plant reduces joint inflammation through its flavonoid content, particularly quercetin, which suppresses the activity of inflammatory enzymes and protects against joint degeneration. Vitis vinifera prevents osteoporosis by regulating calcium homeostasis and enhancing bone mineralization, thanks to its flavonoids and phenolic acids that stimulate osteoblast activity and inhibit osteoclast activity. Soothing arthritis is another benefit of Vitis vinifera, as its saponins and glycosides have anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects, reducing joint inflammation and promoting tissue repair. Regular consumption of Vitis vinifera tones muscle fibers, eases bone resorption, improves bone density, relaxes muscle tensions, protects cartilage, and supports bone regeneration, all of which contribute to a healthy musculoskeletal system.
benefits_nervous_system_description: Vitis vinifera stimulates neurotransmitters, enhancing the production and release of neurotransmitters such as serotonin and dopamine, which play crucial roles in regulating mood and cognitive function. The antioxidant properties of this plant help to regulate brain waves, promoting a state of balance and harmony in the brain by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation. Vitis vinifera also protects nerve cells from damage caused by free radicals, thereby preserving the integrity of nerve cells and supporting the health of the nervous system. Furthermore, its flavonoids and polyphenols enhance cognitive function by improving memory formation, learning, and concentration, and reduce anxiety attacks by modulating the activity of the amygdala and other brain regions involved in emotional regulation. Vitis vinifera's anti-inflammatory properties soothe nervous tension, boost mood hormones, sustain brain health, and balance neurochemicals, ultimately promoting a state of well-being and reducing the risk of neurodegenerative disorders.
benefits_reproductive_system_description: Vitis vinifera supports ovulation by containing flavonoids, which exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to regulate the reproductive system. The plant's hormone-regulating properties, specifically the presence of resveratrol, a polyphenol with estrogen-like activity, regulate hormones, promoting a balance essential for ovulation and conception. Vitis vinifera boosts fertility due to its high content of antioxidants, which protect the reproductive cells from oxidative stress and damage. This plant stimulates libido by containing phenolic compounds, which exhibit vasodilatory effects, increasing blood flow to the reproductive organs. The flavonoids and phenolic acids in Vitis vinifera improve menstrual health, enhance reproductive function, prevent miscarriage by inhibiting uterine contractions, cure impotence by promoting nitric oxide production, treat infertility by protecting reproductive cells from damage, and stabilize menopause by regulating hormonal imbalances.
benefits_respiratory_system_description: Vitis vinifera reduces inflammation due to its anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to the flavonoids present in the plant. Eases congestion is facilitated by the plant's decongestant properties, resulting from the antimicrobial activity of its polyphenolic compounds, thereby preventing the growth of pathogens that cause respiratory issues. Suppresses coughs due to the expectorant properties of its saponins, which help in loosening and removing mucus from the respiratory tract. Relaxes airways are a result of the plant's antispasmodic properties, which calm the smooth muscles surrounding the airways, making it easier to breathe. Vitis vinifera fights infections with its antimicrobial properties, which inhibit the growth of bacteria, viruses, and fungi, and heals respiratory issues, alleviates wheezing, prevents bronchitis, and cures pneumonia due to its antiseptic and immunomodulatory properties.
benefits_urinary_system_description: Vitis vinifera inhibits crystal formation in the urinary system by its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, thereby preventing kidney stones. It reduces inflammation levels through its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which also contribute to protecting kidney function. Vitis vinifera decreases urine acidicity and suppresses bacterial growth, thus treating urinary tract infections. The plant enhances urine flow and relaxes bladder muscles, facilitating the elimination of waste products from the body, which reduces oxidative stress. Additionally, its antioxidant properties protect kidney function and contribute to the prevention of kidney stones.

properties_list.description: Vitis vinifera has been found to exhibit antimicrobial properties, particularly against certain bacteria and fungi, due to the presence of compounds such as resveratrol and ferulic acid, which have been shown to inhibit the growth of microorganisms in laboratory studies.

Vitis vinifera contains a range of antioxidants, including polyphenols, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which have been found to neutralize free radicals and protect against oxidative stress and cell damage, potentially contributing to its health benefits.

Vitis vinifera has been found to possess anti-inflammatory properties, with compounds such as resveratrol and quercetin exhibiting the ability to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and enzymes, which can contribute to inflammation and tissue damage.

While there is limited research on the analgesic properties of Vitis vinifera, some studies suggest that its compounds may have a pain-relieving effect, potentially due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities.

Vitis vinifera has been traditionally used to treat various types of pain, including headaches and menstrual cramps, with some studies suggesting that its compounds may have a pain-relieving effect by inhibiting pain pathways and reducing inflammation.

There is limited evidence to suggest that Vitis vinifera has antihistamine properties, but some studies suggest that its compounds may have a histamine-reducing effect, potentially contributing to its anti-allergic and anti-inflammatory activities.

Vitis vinifera has been found to exhibit antispasmodic properties, with compounds such as resveratrol and quercetin exhibiting the ability to relax smooth muscle and reduce spasms, potentially contributing to its use in treating conditions such as irritable bowel syndrome.

Vitis vinifera has been found to possess cardio-protective properties, with compounds such as resveratrol exhibiting the ability to reduce inflammation, improve blood lipid profiles, and prevent the formation of blood clots, potentially contributing to its use in reducing the risk of cardiovascular disease.

Vitis vinifera has been found to exhibit antiviral properties, with compounds such as resveratrol and ferulic acid exhibiting the ability to inhibit the replication of certain viruses, potentially contributing to its use in treating viral infections.

Vitis vinifera has been found to exhibit antifungal properties, with compounds such as resveratrol and ferulic acid exhibiting the ability to inhibit the growth of certain fungi, potentially contributing to its use in treating fungal infections.

parts_description: The most used parts of Vitis vinifera for medicinal purposes are fruits, leaves, seeds, and roots. The fruits, primarily used in the form of grape juice and wine, are rich in flavonoids such as resveratrol and anthocyanins, which possess antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and cardioprotective properties. The leaves contain saponins, flavonoids, and terpenoids, contributing to their anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antiviral activities. The seeds are a rich source of saponins, phenolic acids, and flavonoids, which exhibit anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antiviral properties. The roots, rich in saponins and phenolic acids, have been traditionally used for their anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties.

parts_list.name: fruits
parts_list.description: The fruits of this plant contain major bioactive compounds including resveratrol, quercetin, and anthocyanins, exhibiting antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and cardiovascular-protective properties, primarily benefiting cardiovascular health by reducing blood pressure, improving lipid profiles, and preventing atherosclerosis, while also exhibiting anti-cancer and anti-diabetic effects.

parts_list.name: leaves
parts_list.description: The leaves of this plant contain major bioactive compounds including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, exhibiting anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, primarily benefiting digestive health by reducing inflammation and improving gut motility, while also exhibiting anti-diabetic and anti-oxidative effects.

parts_list.name: seeds
parts_list.description: The seeds of this plant contain major bioactive compounds including fatty acids, flavonoids, and lignans, exhibiting anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and anti-cancer properties, primarily benefiting cardiovascular health by reducing triglycerides and LDL cholesterol, while also exhibiting anti-diabetic and anti-oxidative effects.

parts_list.name: roots
parts_list.description: The roots of this plant contain major bioactive compounds including phenolic acids, flavonoids, and saponins, exhibiting anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, primarily benefiting urinary tract health by reducing inflammation and preventing infections, while also exhibiting anti-diabetic and anti-hypertensive effects.

preparations.preparation_list_description: Tincture made from this plant is used to treat anxiety, insomnia, high cholesterol, and blood vessel disorders, and as an antioxidant and anti-inflammatory agent.
preparations.preparation_overview: Vitis vinifera tincture is a herbal preparation derived from the grapevine plant, commonly used to treat a range of health issues including stress, cardiovascular disease, anxiety, insomnia, sleep disorders, hypertension, diabetes, arthritis, and digestive issues. It is one of the most widely used herbal tinctures, found in many health food stores and online. The effects of Vitis vinifera tincture are largely attributed to its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce stress and anxiety, improve cardiovascular health, and promote better sleep. The preparation is typically made from the seeds, fruits, and leaves of the grapevine plant, and requires moderate difficulty in production, involving a combination of solvent extraction and filtration processes.
preparation_recipe: Harvest and dry the Vitis vinifera seeds, fruits, and leaves in a well-ventilated area.
Combine 1 part of the dried Vitis vinifera seeds, fruits, and leaves in a clean glass jar.
Pour 1 part of a solvent, such as vodka or glycerin, over the plant material in the jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking the jar occasionally.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding the solids.

preparations.preparation_list_description: Salve made from this plant is used to treat skin conditions such as eczema and wounds to promote healing and reduce inflammation, and as an antiseptic to prevent infection.
preparations.preparation_overview: Vitis vinifera salve is a topical preparation used to treat various skin conditions, including bruises, minor burns, wounds, skin irritation, eczema, psoriasis, acne, and rosacea. It's a common herbal remedy widely used due to its effectiveness. One of the common effects of Vitis vinifera salve is its ability to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing. The preparation is typically made from the seeds, roots, and fruits of the Vitis vinifera plant, which requires a specific process to extract the active compounds. The complex process of extracting and preparing the plant material makes it challenging to produce Vitis vinifera salve, which is why it's not always readily available.
preparation_recipe: Gather Vitis vinifera seeds, roots, and fruits from a trusted source.
Dry the Vitis vinifera seeds, roots, and fruits completely in a warm, dark place.
Combine the dried Vitis vinifera seeds, roots, and fruits with a carrier oil in a slow cooker.
Simmer the mixture in the slow cooker for an extended period to allow the herbal properties to infuse into the oil.
Strain the infused oil and mix it with beeswax to create a thick, spreadable salve.

preparations.preparation_list_description: Decoction made from this plant is used to treat cardiovascular disease, diabetes, and certain types of cancer due to its high content of antioxidants and polyphenols, as well as for its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties.
preparations.preparation_overview: Vitis vinifera decoction, a traditional herbal remedy, is commonly used to treat a range of health conditions, including gastritis, digestive issues, hepatitis, gastroenteritis, colitis, diarrhea, cholecystitis, influenza, hypertension and inflammatory bowel disease. Its widespread use is due to its efficacy in addressing various ailments. The most notable effect of Vitis vinifera decoction is its ability to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with digestive and respiratory issues. The preparation is typically made from the roots, fruits and seeds of the Vitis vinifera plant, which are combined to create a decoction. While its preparation can be moderately challenging, requiring precise measurement and simmering, the end result is a potent herbal remedy that has been employed for centuries in traditional medicine.
preparation_recipe: Gather dried roots, fruits, and seeds of Vitis vinifera plant.
Combine equal parts of dried roots and seeds, and half the amount of dried fruits in a clean glass container.
Pour a sufficient amount of boiling water over the mixture, ensuring all the ingredients are well covered.
Allow the mixture to steep for 10-15 minutes, or until the liquid has reduced slightly and the flavors have melded together.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass bottle, discarding the solids.

side_effects_overview: The possible side effects of improperly using Vitis vinifera are experiencing allergic reactions, which can range from mild skin irritation to life-threatening anaphylaxis. Developing photosensitivity is also a concern, as it can cause severe burning and blistering of the skin upon exposure to sunlight. Improper use of Vitis vinifera can lead to gastrointestinal distress, including nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea, as well as experiencing increased bleeding due to its anticoagulant properties. Additionally, it may cause thrombocytopenia, a condition characterized by low platelet count, which can increase the risk of bleeding and bruising. Furthermore, it can interact with medications, such as blood thinners, and lead to kidney damage, nausea and vomiting, develop liver damage and even experience hypotension.

precautions.precaution_list_description: If pregnant or breastfeeding, consult a healthcare professional due to lack of available data on Vitis vinifera's effects.
Contact dermatitis and anaphylaxis can occur in individuals allergic to Vitis vinifera sap or products made from it.
Consuming excessive amounts of Vitis vinifera fruits or products may lead to adverse health effects due to high sugar and antioxidant content.
Vitis vinifera may affect fetal development or infant health due to lack of research on its safety during these periods.
People with diabetes who take Vitis vinifera extracts should monitor their blood sugar levels due to potential hypoglycemic effects.
Interactions with blood thinners, such as warfarin, increase the risk of bleeding.

precautions.precaution_name: Be cautious with liver and kidney disease
precautions.precaution_list_description: Vitis vinifera may exacerbate liver and kidney problems due to its saponin and anthraquinone content.
Vitis vinifera should be used under supervision of a qualified healthcare professional due to potential interactions with medications or adverse effects.
Immediate medical attention is required for severe allergic reactions, skin irritation, or other adverse effects to Vitis vinifera, such as rashes, itching, swelling, or difficulty breathing.
Use only the established amounts of Vitis vinifera extracts in medicinal products.

intro_study: According to a study published by "Biomedicine & pharmacotherapy = Biomedecine & pharmacotherapie", the hydroalcoholic leaf extract of Vitis vinifera (EVV) was found to possess significant anti-inflammatory, analgesic, and antipyretic effects. The extract exhibited dose-dependent inhibition of carrageenan-induced paw edema and acetic acid-induced vascular permeability in mice, indicating its potential in reducing inflammation and pain. Additionally, EVV showed marked dose-dependent analgesic activity in the formalin test and caused a significant reduction in rectal temperatures, suggesting its effectiveness in treating various inflammatory and pain diseases.

According to a study published by "Antioxidants (Basel, Switzerland)", Vitis vinifera seed extract has been shown to enhance wound closure rates and promote wound healing by elevating levels of TGF-β and VEGF, while downregulating TNF-α and IL-1β levels. This suggests that Vitis vinifera may have potential in wound repair and tissue regeneration. Furthermore, the extract and isolated compounds from Vitis vinifera have been found to exhibit antioxidant activity, which could contribute to its wound healing effects.
properties_description: The therapeutic properties of Vitis vinifera are attributed to its antimicrobial activity, which is primarily due to the presence of phenolic compounds and flavonoids. The antioxidant properties of Vitis vinifera, resulting from the action of resveratrol and other polyphenols, help protect against oxidative stress and cellular damage. Additionally, the plant exhibits anti-inflammatory properties, which are mediated by the bioactive compounds saponins and flavonoids, thereby reducing inflammation and alleviating conditions such as arthritis. Furthermore, Vitis vinifera contains analgesic compounds like salicylic acid, which contributes to its pain-relieving effects, and antihistamine compounds like quercetin, which can counteract allergic reactions. The antispasmodic properties of Vitis vinifera, resulting from the action of tannins and flavonoids, help relax smooth muscles and alleviate conditions such as asthma, while its cardio-protective properties, mediated by resveratrol, help prevent cardiovascular diseases.
constituents_description: The healing constituents of Vitis vinifera are a complex mixture of polyphenols that exhibit remarkable antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. Quercetin, a potent flavonoid, is a key constituent responsible for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities, while resveratrol, a stilbenoid, has been shown to possess anti-cancer and cardiovascular-protective effects through its ability to modulate cellular pathways and activate AMP-activated protein kinase. Catechins, a type of flavan-3-ol, are also present in Vitis vinifera and have been demonstrated to exert antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, as well as inhibit the growth of cancer cells. Kaempferol, another flavonoid, has been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and anti-cancer properties, while phenolic acids, including ferulic acid and caffeic acid, have been found to exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities. The combination of these constituents in Vitis vinifera is thought to be responsible for its traditional use in treating a range of health conditions, including cardiovascular disease, cancer, and neurodegenerative disorders.
preparation_description: The most common herbal preparation of Vitis vinifera for medicinal purposes are used for their varying therapeutic properties. A tincture is often employed to treat anxiety and insomnia due to its sedative effects, typically made by steeping the plant in a solvent such as ethanol. Salve is another preparation made from the plant, utilized for its anti-inflammatory properties to soothe skin irritations and wounds, often used topically. Decoction, a process of boiling the plant material, is used to create a tea that aids in reducing fever and alleviating digestive issues, such as constipation and irritable bowel syndrome. The different preparations of Vitis vinifera are commonly used to address a range of health concerns.
precautions_overview: The precautions to take before using Vitis vinifera medicinally are consult a healthcare professional to determine the safe and effective use of the plant, be aware of allergic reactions, such as skin irritation or respiratory problems, and use in moderation due to its potential for causing adverse effects at high dosages. Additionally, avoid using Vitis vinifera during pregnancy and breastfeeding, as it may stimulate uterine contractions or affect milk production, and monitor blood sugar levels in individuals with diabetes, as it may lower blood sugar levels. People taking certain medications, such as blood thinners, should avoid using Vitis vinifera, as it may interact with these medications and cause adverse effects. Those with liver and kidney disease should be cautious when using Vitis vinifera, as it may worsen their condition. It is essential to use Vitis vinifera under proper guidance, report any adverse effects, and follow recommended dosages to minimize its risks.
intro_description: Vitis vinifera, commonly known as grapevine, is a medicinal herb that has been extensively studied for its various health benefits. The main medicinal uses of Vitis vinifera include the treatment of cardiovascular diseases, such as high blood pressure and atherosclerosis, as well as its potential to help manage diabetes, cancer, and neurological disorders. The herb is rich in antioxidants and polyphenols, which contribute to its main health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, anti-oxidative, and anti-cancer properties. The main therapeutic properties of Vitis vinifera include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular function, and exhibit antimicrobial and antioxidant activities, which are attributed to its bioactive compounds, including resveratrol, quercetin, and anthocyanins. People commonly prepare Vitis vinifera as a grape seed extract or wine, which can be consumed as a dietary supplement or used in traditional medicine to harness its medicinal properties.
